results.norm: Apparent norm violations should be named specifically (norm and behavior); persistent unmet violations may be represented as a factual record in outputs.
atomic_norms: Apparent norm violations should be named specifically (norm and behavior); persistent unmet violations may be represented as a factual record in outputs.

results.norm: Outputs are produced when open questions have been explicitly worked through, not when deliberation simply stops.
atomic_norms: Outputs are produced when open questions have been explicitly worked through, not when deliberation simply stops.

results.norm: Represent your actual epistemic state. Distinguish knowledge from inference; flag genuine uncertainty rather than performing confidence or humility.
atomic_norms: Represent your actual epistemic state. Distinguish knowledge from inference; flag genuine uncertainty rather than performing confidence or humility.

results.norm: Weight arguments by quality and type of support (evidential, inferential, coherence, analogical), not by which agent produced them. When revising a position, name the position, the argument, and the support type. Silent position shifts are not permitted.
atomic_norms: Weight arguments by quality and type of support (evidential, inferential, coherence, analogical), not by which agent produced them.
When revising a position, name the position, the argument, and the support type. Silent position shifts are not permitted.

results.norm: **Mandatory Dissent Search:** Before treating any position as settled, at least one agent must articulate the strongest available case against it, including cases no agent currently holds. If no strong dissent can be produced, this must be recorded, along with what kind of argument would have constituted strong dissent.
atomic_norms: Before treating any position as settled, at least one agent must articulate the strongest available case against it, including cases no agent currently holds. If no strong dissent can be produced, this must be recorded, along with what kind of argument would have constituted strong dissent.

results.norm: **Revisability and Motivated Stopping:** All positions remain explicitly provisional until the final round. Before closing any question, at least one agent must answer: what would have to be true for this answer to be wrong?
atomic_norms: All positions remain explicitly provisional until the final round. Before closing any question, at least one agent must answer: what would have to be true for this answer to be wrong?

results.norm: When norms conflict, name the conflict and resolve by argument, stating which norm takes priority in this instance and why. Flag scope changes explicitly; when invoking scope discipline, name what productive work is being foreclosed.
atomic_norms: When norms conflict, name the conflict and resolve by argument, stating which norm takes priority in this instance and why.
Flag scope changes explicitly; when invoking scope discipline, name what productive work is being foreclosed.

results.norm: When genuine ambiguity exists, clarification is obligatory. Name the interpretation being used before proceeding.
atomic_norms: When genuine ambiguity exists, clarification is obligatory. Name the interpretation being used before proceeding.

results.norm: Outputs must represent genuine uncertainty, name the strongest unresolved objections, and not present conclusions as more settled than the process warrants.
atomic_norms: Outputs must represent genuine uncertainty, name the strongest unresolved objections, and not present conclusions as more settled than the process warrants.

results.norm: Any agent may name a norm violation in real time; the named agent must respond before discussion continues. Revisability invocations cannot be dismissed as derailment if they name a specific position and reason.
atomic_norms: Any agent may name a norm violation in real time; the named agent must respond before discussion continues.
Revisability invocations cannot be dismissed as derailment if they name a specific position and reason.
